newsSinceJun6: Shares fell ~3.4% Tuesday on SpaceX IPO capital-rotation fears (SpaceX begins Nasdaq trading June 12 at ~$135 target), a broader tech selloff, and another next-gen Roadster demo delay to August - flow/sentiment-driven, not robotics-fundamental. Incremental Optimus news June 9: Tesla is ending Model S/X production and converting the Fremont line for Optimus Gen 3 low-volume production targeted summer 2026 (high volume 2027); still no announced external customers. Offsetting positives since June 6: JPMorgan upgrade citing 'physical AI' and May China retail sales +22% / exports +68%.

sources.label: GuruFocus - Tesla falls on SpaceX IPO rotation fears
Capital.com - Tesla stock forecast / SpaceX listing June 9 2026
TechTimes - Fremont Model S line converting to Optimus Gen 3 factory (June 9, 2026)
Motley Fool - Should you wait to buy Tesla after June 12 (SpaceX IPO)

verdicts.rankRationale: Today's -3.4% is flow-driven (SpaceX IPO rotation, Roadster slip) with the Optimus thesis intact, and the Fremont Gen 3 line conversion is real but well-telegraphed commercialization evidence; on a page that penalizes obvious, crowded humanoid narratives, a $1.49T mega-cap dipping 3% does not become hidden alpha. Hold, consistent with yesterday's verdict, with a small positive delta for the factory-conversion confirmation.
verdicts.bull: Fremont Model S line conversion makes Optimus Gen 3 low-volume production a 2026 reality with 2027 scale-up, and JPMorgan's 'physical AI' upgrade plus China retail +22% show the core business stabilizing while the SpaceX-driven dip resets entry.
verdicts.neutral: Tesla remains the index-level expression of humanoids - essential beta, not alpha; incremental Optimus capacity news was widely expected and no external customers exist yet, so the page-specific setup is unchanged.
verdicts.bear: Musk conceded Optimus is 'not in usage in our factories in a material way,' Figure/Unitree/BYD are scaling cheaper and faster, and the June 12 SpaceX listing could structurally divert retail and institutional flows that have propped up Tesla's robotics premium.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Optimus Gen 3 low-volume production start at Fremont (summer 2026) and any first external customer announcement.
verdicts.primaryRisk: SpaceX Nasdaq debut June 12 siphoning Tesla's narrative capital, plus continued absence of material Optimus deployment evidence at a 350x+ P/E.
